title: gladiolus involutus
intro: Gladiolus involutus, also known as the lesser gladiolus, is a perennial plant species native to Southern Africa, characterized by its small, trumpet-shaped flowers and linear leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Gladiolus involutus include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to aid in digestion. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
